text: The surgeon takes an anchoring stitch at the near end of the cystic duct stump and begins a series of running throws, ensuring each stitch is snug with small interval bites approximately two millimeters apart, progressing towards the far end.

The surgeon has divided the neck and cystic duct junction, with tissue retracted properly. The surgeon returns to the starting point to take an interlock stitch and uses scissors in the mega suture card to divide the remaining tissue.
